title: Simultaneous determination of praseodymium, neodymium, holmium and erbium in rare earth mixtures with ofloxacin
author: Wang Naixing
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Shandong University, Jinan, 250100, China 

 Shandong Province City Building School, China 

  Manuscript received 30 September 1999, revised 3 February 2000, accepted 28 April 2000  

 A new reagent system has been developed for the simultaneous determination of praseodymium neodimium,holmium and erbium in mixed rare earths. The method is based on the absorption spectra of 4 f -electron transitions of Pr, Nd, Ho and Er complexes with ofloxacin. The second derivative spectrum has been used to eliminate the interference and to improve the sensitivity by a further factor of 2-7. Beer's law is obeyed for 7-50 ug ml -1  of Pr, Nd, Ho and Er. Their relative standard deviation of the absorption signals are found to be 3.8, 2.2, 2.6 and 3.2%, respectively.
